# A4.3 Machine Learning Approaches (HL only)


## A4.3.1 Linear Regression


### A4.3.1.1 Definition and Purpose


Linear regression is a fundamental supervised learning technique used to predict continuous numerical outcomes. It establishes a linear relationship between input variables (features) and output variables (targets).

<callout type=""></callout>

  <callout type=""></callout>
  <callout type=""></callout>
  <callout type=""></callout>

### A4.3.1.2 Key Components


The linear regression equation is represented as:  
$$ y = mx + b $$  
where:

  * m is the slope (coefficient)
  * b is the y-intercept
  * x is the input variable
  * y is the predicted output

<callout type=""></callout>

### A4.3.1.3 Model Evaluation


Common metrics for evaluating linear regression models:

  1. R-squared (R²):
      * Ranges from 0 to 1
    * Higher values indicate better fit
    * Formula: $R^2 = 1 - \frac{\sum(y_i - \hat{y_i})^2}{\sum(y_i - \bar{y})^2}$
  2. Mean Squared Error (MSE):  
$$ MSE = \frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^{n}(y_i - \hat{y_i})^2 $$
  3. Mean Absolute Error (MAE):  
$$ MAE = \frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^{n}|y_i - \hat{y_i}| $$

## A4.3.2 Classification


### A4.3.2.1 Definition and Purpose


Classification is used to predict discrete categorical outcomes, unlike regression which predicts continuous values.

<callout type=""></callout>

  <callout type=""></callout>
  <callout type=""></callout>

### A4.3.2.2 Algorithms


#### K-Nearest Neighbours (K-NN)


  * Works by finding K closest data points to make predictions
  * Distance typically measured using Euclidean distance:  
$$ d = \sqrt{\sum_{i=1}^{n}(x_i - y_i)^2} $$

<callout type=""></callout>

  <callout type=""></callout>
  <callout type=""></callout>

#### Decision Trees


  * Tree-like structure of decisions based on feature values
  * Splits data based on information gain or Gini impurity

<callout type=""></callout>

### A4.3.2.3 Model Evaluation


Key metrics for classification:

  1. Accuracy = $\frac{\text{Correct predictions}}{\text{Total predictions}}$
  2. Precision = $\frac{\text{True Positives}}{\text{True Positives + False Positives}}$
  3. Recall = $\frac{\text{True Positives}}{\text{True Positives + False Negatives}}$
  4. F1 Score = $2 \times \frac{\text{Precision} \times \text{Recall}}{\text{Precision} + \text{Recall}}$
